page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 180 GB of dedicated RAM, providing 3.75 GB per core. Storage is bundled directly, consisting of eight local NVMe SSDs that deliver a total capacity of 2951 GB. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier status in single-threaded gzip compression (37.09 GB/sec), single-threaded bzip3 decompression (15.63 GB/sec), and Geekbench single-core execution (2309.0). Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 3861.88 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 178.28 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is solid, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score of 70646.91 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 19360.0. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and large-block memory write speeds fall into the average tier.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations. These hardware accelerators contribute to strong performance in LLM inference, where prompt processing speeds remain in the top 25% across small, medium, and large models. The server is highly effective for static web serving, reaching a top-tier throughput of 83.58 GB/sec, and handles database operations efficiently, as shown by 89.08 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. The combination of high-speed local NVMe storage and compute density makes this instance suitable for database hosting, software compilation, and localized machine learning inference.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48-lssd is a compute-focused server featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 180 GB of RAM and 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across eight drives. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive top-tier single-core benchmarks, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2309.0 and 37.09 GB/sec in single-threaded gzip compression. While multi-core raw compute and large-block memory write speeds sit in the average tier, the server excels in memory read bandwidth and database operations, processing 89.08 million Redis SET operations per second. This hardware profile is optimized for high-throughput database workloads, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48-lssd features 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance and high-throughput database capabilities.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48-lssd is a 48-vCPU server with 180 GB RAM and 2951 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for databases and compute.
tagline: A high-compute server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, rapid local NVMe storage, and advanced matrix extensions for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across eight bundled drives
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Top-tier single-core performance and memory read bandwidth
Suited for database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation
